spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates the `corner-shape` property with a live `superellipse(n)` exponent slider that morphs the same border-radius from circle to scoop to notch â€” the spec's headline curvature continuum.

interactive.rationale: Working range input with 2 live listeners + a feature-detect probe, driving a real `corner-shape` CSS value as the user drags.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index says `Drag the superellipse curvature and watch the same border-radius produce circle, squircle, scoop, or notch` â€” page is exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: Slider covers the canonical superellipse range but doesn't show the named-value shortcuts (`squircle`, `scoop`, `bevel`, `notch`, `straight`) the spec also accepts in parallel, nor what happens at exponent values outside the typical 0.5â€“4 range.

references.rationale: `see also` cites the chromestatus link and the css-borders-4 corner-shaping anchor.

design_system.rationale: 47 CSS variable references, no raw hex, no button override mismatch.

openQuestions.title: Pair the slider with named-value snap points
openQuestions.detail: The spec accepts both `superellipse(n)` and named values (`squircle`, `scoop`, `bevel`, `notch`, `straight`, `round`). Add a row of snap buttons that jump the slider to the equivalent exponent so authors learn the named-value vocabulary alongside the continuum.

openQuestions.title: Show the spec's edge values
openQuestions.detail: What happens at `superellipse(0)` (straight cut) or very large N (effectively a square)? Either clamp the slider to those endpoints with labels or surface what the browser renders so the spec's range is documented.

openQuestions.title: Compare with `border-radius` only
openQuestions.detail: Add a side-by-side rendering of the same border-radius without `corner-shape` so visitors see what the new property is doing on top of the existing one â€” currently the demo is solo.

summary: Solid headline-knob demo of the new property with a real live slider and proper references. Pairing the continuum with named-value snap buttons and a `border-radius alone` baseline would convert it from `feel the curvature` to a complete spec teach.
